text: SitRep58 Tableau 7: 753 in isolation at end of day (248 confirmed / 505 suspected, suspected reconciled to the census since the source dont-rows 248+539 overcount vs the 753 census on Nord-Kivu) at 93.9% global bed occupancy; Nord-Kivu 129.6% saturation, Ituri 87%, Sud-Kivu 66.6%. Exits over 24h (110 total): 15 deaths, 75 non-cases, 12 recoveries, 8 escapes; 94 admissions.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Recovered advanced to 318 DRC cumulative (+12). J-1 bed count is 769; the Tableau-7 end-of-day census is 753.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: 53 new confirmed cases and 30 deaths (66.6% community) were reported for 2026-07-11, in Ituri (45 cases) and Nord-Kivu (8 cases). Confirmed cases rose to 1926 and deaths to 702; the global CFR is 36.4%.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: SitRep 58 folds Haut-Uele (Wamba) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani, Mangobo, Lubunga) into the national total for the first time (previously starred/excluded), and adds Ariwara as a newly affected Ituri health zone; the outbreak now spans five provinces and 42 health zones.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Nord-Kivu CTE occupancy is 129.6% and global bed occupancy is 93.9% (753 in isolation, 248 confirmed / 505 suspected). 18 UNICEF motorcycles and 10 tonnes of PCI/PECH supplies were received; 25 vehicles and 5 ambulances are available across pillars.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Ituri concentrates the epidemic at 1745 confirmed / 601 deaths, CFR 34.4%. Bunia (507), Rwampara (393), Mongbwalu (336), Nizi (121) and Nyankunde (99) remain the most affected zones; the epidemic is currently progressing fastest in Nizi.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Nord-Kivu totals 173 confirmed / 97 deaths, CFR 56.1%, carried by Katwa (66.2%), Beni (66.7%) and Butembo (47.7%); Katwa (71), Butembo (44) and Beni (30) lead notification.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Sud-Kivu has passed 42+ consecutive days without a new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26), holding in the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration; 30 patients remain in isolation.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: reticence_post_mortem - Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities. - Response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: case_load_and_capacity - Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation at 129.6%; national isolation occupancy is 93.9% (753 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk. - Response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: contact_followup_gap - Contact follow-up reached 78.3% versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted. - Response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: weak_alert_reporting - Weak alert investigation in Ituri (73.9% investigation rate) risks undetected, silent transmission. - Response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: unlocalized_confirmed_cases - Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction. - Response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_medicines_gap - Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management. - Response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ipc_and_hcw_infections - Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk among health workers (112 HCW cases, 35 deaths, CFR 31.2%). - Response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: lab_backlog - A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 56.3% provincial CFR. - Response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ambulance_isolation_gap - Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk. - Response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: coordination_gap - Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions. - Response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: financial_resource_gap - A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars. - Response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: insecurity_access - Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones. - Response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_services_continuity - Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality. - Response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: population_mobility - High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; two Kisangani (Tshopo) detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) are under investigation and 159912 travelers passed through PoE/PoC this cycle. - Response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: nord_kivu_lab_logistics - Nord-Kivu reports a laboratory backlog / delayed diagnosis (5.6% positivity) and, in its logistics subset, no functional ambulance or hearse; Mutwanga and Kasindi-Lubiriha flag insufficient IPC inputs and swabs for community-death alerts. - Response: Clear the laboratory backlog, restore transport/EDS logistics, and resupply IPC inputs and swabs to the affected Nord-Kivu health zones.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Source review / Before zone-level attribution use: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones, and confirm the SR58 accounting change that folds Haut-Uele (Wamba) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani, Mangobo, Lubunga) into the national total, plus the newly affected Ariwara zone.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Case management / Urgent: Relieve isolation load at 93.9% national occupancy and Nord-Kivu 129.6% saturation (56.1% provincial CFR); reconcile the Nord-Kivu confirmed/suspected isolation split and accelerate CTE construction/expansion.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Surveillance / Ongoing: Improve contact follow-up (78.3% vs 95% target), raise Ituri alert investigation above 73.9%, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog / delayed diagnosis.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Sud-Kivu transition / Residual-surveillance phase: Sustain residual epidemiological surveillance in Sud-Kivu (42+ days with no new confirmed case) toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration, while resolving the 30 patients still in isolation.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.
